sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel data documented for this NES title

special_mechanics.name: Bubble Projectile
special_mechanics.description: Milon shoots bubbles as primary weapon and tool. Bubbles can destroy soft blocks to reveal routes and items, or eliminate enemies (which respawn after several seconds). Bubbles can be upgraded to fly further and larger after defeating bosses.

special_mechanics.name: Room-Based Navigation
special_mechanics.description: Game uses non-linear room structure with fixed single exits. Unlike traditional left-to-right platformers, rooms have free-flowing layouts accessible in any order.

tier_notes: Tier 2 empirical/qualitative data. No numerical physics parameters documented in available sources. Movement mechanics inferred from gameplay descriptions only: player runs, jumps, and shoots bubbles; no specific velocity or acceleration values provided. Game notable for lack of invincibility frames on damage and one-life design.
sources_used: Wikipedia - Milon's Secret Castle (gameplay and mechanics sections)
